wplace.title: Why creators use Pixel Art Village
wplace.desc: Pixel Art Village makes the core image to pixel art workflow simple: upload an image, tune the look, and export a clean image result without leaving the browser. Pixel Art Village keeps every image step in one place.

free.title: 100% Free
free.desc: Use the editor, palettes, and exports for free. No account, credit card, or download limit.

privacy.title: Privacy First (Client‑Side)
privacy.desc: All processing happens in your browser. Your images stay on your device unless you choose to share them.

easy.title: Easy for Everyone
easy.desc: The editor is built for quick iteration, so you can test image styles, palettes, and sizes without a steep learning curve.

size.title: No Dimension Limits
size.desc: Work with anything from small icons to large reference images, then scale the final image export for your project.

features.title: Pixel Art Converter Controls
features.desc: This image to pixel art converter lets you control how your image looks with palette, pixel size, dithering, and cleanup settings before you export.

palette.title: Custom palette
palette.desc: Switch between built-in palettes or create your own to match a retro system, a game project, or an image style.

live.title: Live adjustments
live.desc: Change pixel size, brightness, contrast, saturation, and dithering and see the image result update immediately in the image preview.

clientSide.title: Private processing
clientSide.desc: Your image stays in the browser while you edit, preview, and export.

how.title: How to Convert Image to Pixel Art

steps.download.desc: Export the result as PNG, JPEG, or WebP for sprites, icons, UI art, or retro graphics.

overview.title: Overview
overview.p1: pixel art Village respects your privacy. This policy explains what data we process, how we use cookies (including Google AdSense cookies), and your choices. If you have questions, please contact us via the email on the Contact page.

local.title: Local Image Processing
local.p1: Your images never leave your device. All image processing happens locally in your browser. We do not upload, store, or share your image files on our servers.

collect.title: Information We Collect
collect.li1: Technical data for site operation (e.g., basic device/browser info) necessary to deliver the service.
collect.li2: Usage data in aggregate to improve features (if analytics is enabled, we avoid collecting personally identifiable information).
collect.li3: Advertising data from Google AdSense (see below) if ads are displayed.
collect.p2: We do not require account registration. We do not knowingly collect sensitive personal data.

cookies.title: Cookies and AdSense
cookies.p1: We may use cookies and similar technologies to operate the website and improve your experience. If Google AdSense is enabled, Google and its partners may use cookies to serve ads based on your visits to this and other sites.
cookies.learn: Learn how Google uses information from sites or apps that use Google services:
cookies.more: More on Google ads and cookies:

third.title: Third‑Party Partners
third.p1: We may integrate third‑party services (e.g., Google AdSense). These partners may process data under their own privacy policies. We encourage you to review their policies to understand data handling practices.

use.title: Data Use and Sharing
use.li2: Safeguard service integrity, detect abuse, and improve features.
use.li3: Comply with legal obligations. We do not sell personal data.

rights.title: Your Choices and Rights
rights.li1: Opt‑out or manage ad personalization using the links above.
rights.li2: Control cookies via your browser settings.
rights.li3: Contact us to exercise applicable privacy rights (access, correction, deletion as required by law).

children.title: Children’s Privacy
children.p1: The service is not directed to children under 13 (or the age required by local law). We do not knowingly collect personal information from children.

changes.title: Changes to this Policy
changes.p1: We may update this policy from time to time. Material changes will be highlighted on this page with an updated date. Your continued use of the site indicates acceptance of the updated policy.

contact.title: Contact
contact.p1: For privacy inquiries, please see the Contact page.

privacy.seoDesc: pixel art Village privacy policy: local image processing, AdSense cookies, third‑party partners, your choices and rights, contact info.

use.title: Use of the Service

use.li2: Do not abuse, interfere with, or disrupt the Service or other users.
use.li3: Do not use the Service for illegal, harmful, or infringing activities.

ip.title: Intellectual Property
ip.p1: We retain all rights to the Service and related materials. You retain rights to your original uploads and generated outputs, subject to your applicable rights and third‑party rights.

processing.title: Image Processing
processing.p1: Image processing is performed locally in your browser. We do not store your uploaded images on our servers.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer of Warranties
disclaimer.p1: The Service is provided “as is” and “as available” without warranties of any kind, express or implied. We do not warrant that the Service will be uninterrupted or error‑free.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.p1: To the maximum extent permitted by law, we shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from or related to your use of the Service.

changes.title: Changes to the Service or Terms
changes.p1: We may modify or discontinue the Service and update these Terms at any time. Continued use of the Service constitutes acceptance of updated Terms.

law.title: Governing Law
law.p1: These Terms are governed by applicable laws in your jurisdiction to the extent not preempted by other laws. Venue and jurisdiction will follow applicable conflict of laws rules.

contact.title: Contact
contact.p1: For questions about these Terms, please see the Contact page.

items.question: Is this image to pixel art tool free to use?
items.answer: Yes. This image to pixel art tool is free. You can upload an image, adjust the image settings, and download the image result for free.

items.question: Do you upload images to a server?
items.answer: No. This image to pixel art tool runs in your browser, so your source image and final image stay on your device.

items.question: Can I convert PNG, JPG, GIF, or photo files here?
items.answer: Yes. You can convert PNG, JPG, GIF, and WEBP image files here, and you can open a dedicated photo workflow from the homepage.

items.question: Is this an AI pixel art generator?
items.answer: No. This page is a rule-based image to pixel art tool, not an AI pixel art generator, so you keep direct control over the source image, palette, and pixel settings.

items.question: How do I get cleaner pixel art results?
items.answer: Start with a clear source image, reduce extra detail, then convert image to pixel art with the palette and pixel size that best match your final export.

items.question: Is this also a pixel art maker?
items.answer: Yes. It works well as an image-based pixel art maker and pixel art converter when you want to refine an existing image instead of starting from scratch.

items.question: What is Pixel Art Village?
items.answer: Pixel Art Village is a browser-based pixel art converter hub for image to pixel art conversion, photo cleanup, sprite refinement, and retro image export without sending files to a server. Pixel Art Village keeps processing private and direct.

items.question: Should I start with the main converter or a dedicated page?
items.answer: Start with the main image to pixel art converter for general image work. This pixel art converter is the right starting point before you open a dedicated page for a photo, PNG, JPG, GIF, or another specific image workflow.

items.question: Does the full editor keep images private?
items.answer: Yes. Image processing happens in your browser, so your image files, image preview, and exported image stay on your device while you preview and export in the pixel art converter.

items.question: Which image file types can I start with?
items.answer: You can start with common browser image formats including PNG, JPG, GIF, and WEBP in Pixel Art Village.
